note: Operator-labeled ground truth for the deterministic video gates. Seeded from the video-aliveness audit. calibrate.ts fetches each entry's PUBLIC poster (+ footage.social.mp4 when --footage is passed, since those files are large), runs the relevant gate, and prints gate-verdict vs operator-verdict so thresholds stay re-earnable as verdicts accumulate. Add rows over time; keep notes concrete. Dimensions: arc (needs footage), diversity (poster-pair, offline).

entries.notes: a real arc — the structural evolution across the clip the arc gate must PASS (wholeClipChange ~0.356)
20s of near-static bars, cool palette — the dead clip the arc gate must FAIL (wholeClipChange ~0.220)

uniform wallpaper, cool palette — an AESTHETIC/monotony rejection, NOT structural deadness: the arc gate correctly reads it evolving (~0.511). Kept here as a documented coverage boundary — this failure mode belongs to the LLM judge (or a future texture-variety/palette metric), not the deterministic arc floor. calibrate prints its arc score for transparency but does not score it.
the same primitive as 025.5.5T recolored — a diversity repaint; the immediate-neighbour distance must read TOO SIMILAR
the other half of the repaint pair

CHAPTER-LENGTH coverage boundary (Unit O, the set-video hour render). Provenance: the 019.F.1A pilot rendered 012.2.4L as a 148s set chapter (caustic web) — the sec-driven `rise` ramp rescaled to span the chapter (no 20s freeze), the tail settle suppressed. Rendered stills at 12s/62s/136s are visibly alive (sparse -> flooded -> distinct), yet the deterministic structural-arc gate reads wholeClipChange ~0.231 at chapter length, BELOW the 20s floor of 0.29. This is a false-fail by construction: the arc floor is calibrated for a 20s per-track JOURNEY (depart->arrive structural reorganization), but a minutes-long chapter is a STEADY-STATE slice of one vehicle that floods/churns (brightness + density) without structurally reorganizing. So the per-track structural arc floor does NOT apply to set chapters — the arc gate is ADVISORY at chapter length (render-set treats it so); chapter aliveness is judged off the StudioEnvelope + a visual review, and the strobe/flash gate stays HARD. calibrate fetches 012.2.4L's public 20s footage for transparency but does not score this row (like 027.2.8R).
